title: Logical OR ||
subtitle: One true condition is enough
goal: Learn to use the || operator when at least one true condition is enough.
explanation: It returns true if at least one side is true.
The result is false only when both sides are false.
|| is often used when several alternatives are acceptable.
This operator is very useful for conditions, checks, and fallback values.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: Imagine that either of two keys can open a door. If you have at least one key, you can enter.

remember: || returns true if at least one side is true.
The result is false only when every side is false.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Beginners sometimes think || requires two true values. In fact, just one true value is enough for ||.
